title: Workforms - creating scored surveys and how to share results with participants
excerpt: Hi! I have built out a ‘qualification’ form in our CRM, to ask specific questions on new prospects, and each answer has a weighted score. At the end of the 10/11 questions, I’d really like for whoever is filling out the …

posts.author: nslewis (Nia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! I have built out a ‘qualification’ form in our CRM, to ask specific questions on new prospects, and each answer has a weighted score. At the end of the 10/11 questions, I’d really like for whoever is filling out the form, to see what the total score out of 40 is.

I’m VERY proud that I’ve managed to figure out the formulas for each answer and have that populated in the workform board, along with the total SUM - but now I need a clean way to share that total at the end of each individual survey, if at all possible.

As lots of people need to use this form, ideally it would be something that can appear on the thank you page of the form, so they know straight away, without having to separately dig around on the workform submissions or board on Monday. I’ve seen something about redirecting to a URL but not sure how that would work. Any help is appreciated.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about having the score result emailed to them? You could add an automation that when an item is created (form submitted) that it emails the email column they filled out (then choose the score as part of the email body)

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: nslewis (Nia)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your reply! Yes I did look into this, but because the form is often filled out during meetings and shared with the team ‘live’ as it’s being populated/discussed, I was keen to keep the process and streamlined as possible to save having to dive into another app or system to get the results (we originally had this set up on simple excel where you could see the score as you work through the questions)
